issue_title: Login Back Button Restarts Authentication Flow

reasoning_trace.target: Log In|We'll email|email you a code|authentication|login|sign in|Sign In|Sign in
reasoning_trace.finding: Initial broad search was attempted but rg was unavailable; switched to PowerShell search. The broad recursive search was noisy due to generated/cache content, so analysis narrowed to Simplenote/src/main.

reasoning_trace.target: Simplenote/src/main: We'll email|email you a code|Magic Link|magic link|Log In|log in|login|Authenticate|Authentication
reasoning_trace.finding: Found authentication classes and strings: SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity, SimplenoteSignupActivity, SignInFragment, MagicLinkableFragment, MagicLinkConfirmationFragment, NewCredentialsActivity, and strings including signin_login_with_email_manually.

Identified likely source files for the auth flow: authentication/SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity.java, authentication/SimplenoteSignupActivity.java, authentication/SignInFragment.kt, authentication/MagicLinkableFragment.kt, authentication/magiclink/MagicLinkConfirmationFragment.kt, authentication/NewCredentialsActivity.kt, plus login layouts.

SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity, SimplenoteSignupActivity, and NewCredentialsActivity are declared as activities. DeepLinkActivity handles simplenote login links but is not the reported manual back path.

The initial authentication activity handles the Log In button via buttonLoginClicked -> onLoginSheetEmailClicked, which starts SimplenoteSignupActivity with KEY_IS_LOGIN=true and then calls this.finish().

The login fragment inflates fragment_login, sets the 'signin_login_manually' TextView click listener to showLoginWithPassword(activity, email), and showLoginWithPassword starts NewCredentialsActivity then calls act.finish().
MagicLinkableFragment handles email-code login and, on successful request, adds MagicLinkConfirmationFragment to R.id.fragment_container and adds it to the fragment back stack.

The code confirmation fragment can launch password login through SignInFragment.showLoginWithPassword(activity, username); successful login finishes the activity, but normal back should be handled by the fragment manager.
Found relevant back handling in SimplenoteSignupActivity and NewCredentialsActivity, plus the fragment transaction in MagicLinkableFragment.

The Log In screen contains TextView R.id.sign_in_login_manually below the magic-link button. This is the UI text described by the bug report: 'We'll email you a code to log in, or you can log in manually.'
The code confirmation screen contains its own login button and an 'Enter password' button, but the reproduction's quoted 'We'll email you a code...' text belongs to fragment_login, not this layout.

SimplenoteSignupActivity hosts SignInFragment when KEY_IS_LOGIN is true. Toolbar home calls onBackPressed. Its onBackPressed only delegates to super for MagicLinkConfirmationFragment; otherwise it starts SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity and finishes.
The signup/login host layout has a Toolbar and fragment_container; no layout-level back behavior exists.

Confirmed the initial auth flow: SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity starts SimplenoteSignupActivity for login and finishes itself; SignInFragment starts NewCredentialsActivity for manual/password login and finishes its host.
No local implementation of com.simperium.android.AuthenticationActivity exists in the repo, so only the concrete Simplenote override is available for closed-box analysis.
NewCredentialsActivity is the password Log In screen. Its system/top-app-bar back path calls onBackPressed, and onBackPressed starts SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity then finishes.
SignupFragment uses a replacement transaction to show signup confirmation and is not part of the reproduced login/password path.
Confirmed exact UI text: signin_login_with_email_manually is 'We'll email you a code to log in, or you can log in manually.' and login_screen_title is 'Log In'.
Confirmed the quoted reproduction text is used by fragment_login.xml on the SignInFragment screen.
Confirmed top app bar back in NewCredentialsActivity invokes onBackPressed, so both system navigation and toolbar back run the same restart-auth behavior.

reasoning_trace.target: SignInFragment.showLoginWithPassword calls act.finish() after launching NewCredentialsActivity
reasoning_trace.finding: This destroys the previous Log In fragment host, so pressing back from NewCredentialsActivity cannot reveal the prior login screen; the credentials activity instead restarts SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity.

For the email-code confirmation screen, the fragment is added to the back stack and SimplenoteSignupActivity has a MagicLinkConfirmationFragment branch that delegates to super.onBackPressed, so this is less consistent with the quoted reproduction text.

This fallback can also restart the auth flow, but after the reproduced tap on the 'We'll email...' TextView the active screen is NewCredentialsActivity; the prerequisite fault is that SignInFragment finished the previous activity when launching it.

reasoning_trace.target: Initial auth -> Log In -> SignInFragment TextView -> NewCredentialsActivity -> back
reasoning_trace.finding: SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity.onLoginSheetEmailClicked starts SimplenoteSignupActivity and finishes; SignInFragment.showLoginWithPassword starts NewCredentialsActivity and finishes SimplenoteSignupActivity; NewCredentialsActivity back starts SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity, showing the initial auth screen instead of the previous SignInFragment.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This statement destroys the activity hosting the previous Log In fragment immediately after launching NewCredentialsActivity. When the user presses system or toolbar back on the password Log In screen, there is no previous login screen in the task to return to, so NewCredentialsActivity.onBackPressed starts SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity and the authentication flow appears to restart.

final_answer.causal_chain: After clearing app data, the initial SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity shows the auth chooser. Tapping Log In calls onLoginSheetEmailClicked, starts SimplenoteSignupActivity with KEY_IS_LOGIN=true, and finishes the chooser. SimplenoteSignupActivity shows SignInFragment. Tapping the 'We'll email you a code...' TextView calls SignInFragment.showLoginWithPassword, which starts NewCredentialsActivity and then executes act.finish(), destroying the SignInFragment host. On the password Log In screen, both toolbar back and system back run NewCredentialsActivity.onBackPressed, which starts SimplenoteAuthenticationActivity because the previous SignInFragment activity was already finished. The result is the initial authentication screen instead of the previous login screen.
final_answer.fix_hint: Do not finish the SignInFragment host when launching NewCredentialsActivity for password login; let normal activity back navigation return to the previous Log In screen.
